daily_summary.headline: Exciting Space Events and Aurora Opportunities on the Horizon

top_events.title: Vega-C Launch with SMILE Mission
top_events.description: The Vega-C rocket will launch the Solar wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer (SMILE), a joint mission between ESA and the Chinese Academy of Sciences, to study Earth's magnetosphere.
top_events.action: Watch live stream for insights into Earth's protective shield.

top_events.title: Starship Flight 12 Test Launch
top_events.description: SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 will test new technologies for the Starship reusable launch vehicle, including payload deployment and heat shield analysis.
top_events.action: Follow the test flight for groundbreaking technology demonstrations.

top_events.title: Aurora Viewing Opportunity
top_events.description: A G1 geomagnetic storm may create aurora visible to those at higher latitudes, with the best viewing opportunity on May 19 at 03:00 UTC.
top_events.action: Check skies if you're above 60° latitude for potential aurora sightings.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
